growth_records.agit: 140 rpm (R. sphaeroides microaerobic); 180 rpm (E. coli in LB)
growth_records.cite: Grützner et al. The small DUF1127 protein CcaF1 from Rhodobacter sphaeroides is an RNA-binding protein involved in sRNA maturation and RNA turnover. Nucleic Acids Research (2021). DOI:10.1093/nar/gkab146. [PMC8034643]
growth_records.snippet: Overexpression of CcsRs results in considerably slower growth (doubling time 5.4 h compared to the wild type with 3.5 h), while the doubling time of a strain having CcaF1 with CcsR1–4 together on a plasmid is only slight
growth_records.notes: growth_rate_per_h (mu) = ln(2)/3.5 h = 0.198 h^-1. R. sphaeroides strains routinely cultivated 'in a malate minimal-salt medium ... at 32°C in the dark'; exact oxygen tension (fully aerobic vs the paper's defined 'microaerobic', 25-30 µM dissolved O2) for this particular growth-curve experiment is not explicitly restated at the point of the doubling-time sentence, so aerobic/dark default cultivation condition assumed (confidence downgraded to medium).

growth_records.agit: 140 rpm (R. sphaeroides microaerobic); 180 rpm (E. coli in LB)
growth_records.cite: Grützner et al. The small DUF1127 protein CcaF1 from Rhodobacter sphaeroides is an RNA-binding protein involved in sRNA maturation and RNA turnover. Nucleic Acids Research (2021). DOI:10.1093/nar/gkab146. [PMC8034643]
growth_records.snippet: Overexpression of CcsRs results in considerably slower growth (doubling time 5.4 h compared to the wild type with 3.5 h)
growth_records.notes: growth_rate_per_h (mu) = ln(2)/5.4 h = 0.1284 h^-1. Same base cultivation conditions as wild type (malate minimal-salt medium, 32°C, dark).

growth_records.strain: 2.4.1 with CcaF1 and CcsR1-4 co-expressed on a plasmid
growth_records.agit: 140 rpm (R. sphaeroides microaerobic); 180 rpm (E. coli in LB)
growth_records.cite: Grützner et al. The small DUF1127 protein CcaF1 from Rhodobacter sphaeroides is an RNA-binding protein involved in sRNA maturation and RNA turnover. Nucleic Acids Research (2021). DOI:10.1093/nar/gkab146. [PMC8034643]
growth_records.snippet: the doubling time of a strain having CcaF1 with CcsR1–4 together on a plasmid is only slightly increased (4.0 h) compared to that of the wild type
growth_records.notes: growth_rate_per_h (mu) = ln(2)/4.0 h = 0.1733 h^-1. Same base cultivation conditions as wild type (malate minimal-salt medium, 32°C, dark).

growth_records.agit: 140 rpm (R. sphaeroides microaerobic); 180 rpm (E. coli in LB)
growth_records.cite: Grützner et al. The small DUF1127 protein CcaF1 from Rhodobacter sphaeroides is an RNA-binding protein involved in sRNA maturation and RNA turnover. Nucleic Acids Research (2021). DOI:10.1093/nar/gkab146. [PMC8034643]
growth_records.snippet: Overexpression of ccaF1 alone results in an extended lag phase but growth in exponential phase is not affected (doubling time of 3.4 h).
growth_records.notes: growth_rate_per_h (mu) = ln(2)/3.4 h = 0.2038 h^-1. Exponential-phase doubling time only (lag phase extended but not quantified numerically in this passage); same base cultivation conditions as wild type (malate minimal-salt medium, 32°C, dark).

growth_records.agit: sparged with gas mixtures (rpm not stated)

up.method: model input constraint used in iRsp1140 FBA of SMM growth, 'based on previous measurements (ref 17)'; not measured in this study
model input constraint used in iRsp1140 FBA of SMM growth, 'based on previous measurements (ref 17)'; not measured in this study
free exchange permitted in FBA for aerobic simulation (bounds not fixed)

growth_records.cite: Burger et al. Combining Genome-Scale Experimental and Computational Methods To Identify Essential Genes in Rhodobacter sphaeroides. mSystems (2017). DOI:10.1128/mSystems.00015-17. [PMC5513736]
growth_records.method: not reported (grown ~7 doublings by OD600; no numeric rate or elapsed time given in text)
growth_records.snippet: The uptake rates for the limiting substrates succinate and ammonium were set to 2.5 and 1 mmol gDW-1 h-1, respectively, based on previous measurements (17). For simulation of aerobic growth, the free exchange of oxygen was permitted, while
growth_records.notes: SMM = Sistrom's minimal medium with succinate and ammonium as sole carbon and nitrogen sources (composition cited to ref 62, not given numerically in text). Model (iRsp1140) freely exchanged phosphate, sulfate, magnesium, iron, nicotinate, thiamine, biotin and CO2 to mimic SMM; no concentrations stated -> medium_composition_stated:false. The succinate (2.5) and ammonium (1) mmol/gDW/h uptake fluxes are FBA input constraints taken from prior measurements (ref 17), NOT rates measured in this paper; recorded verbatim, no arithmetic performed. Growth condition: liquid batch, 30C, ~7 doublings. No experimental mu, doubling time or measured secretion flux reported in text.

growth_records.agit: sparged with gas mixtures (rpm not stated)

up.method: model input constraint (limiting substrate) used in iRsp1140 FBA of SMM growth, from prior measurements (ref 17); not measured here
model input constraint (limiting substrate) used in iRsp1140 FBA of SMM growth, from prior measurements (ref 17); not measured here
photon uptake unconstrained in FBA for anaerobic photosynthetic simulation
oxygen uptake set to 0 for anaerobic photosynthetic FBA simulation

growth_records.cite: Burger et al. Combining Genome-Scale Experimental and Computational Methods To Identify Essential Genes in Rhodobacter sphaeroides. mSystems (2017). DOI:10.1128/mSystems.00015-17. [PMC5513736]
growth_records.method: not reported (grown ~7 doublings by OD600; no numeric rate or elapsed time given in text)
growth_records.snippet: Conversely, for simulation of anaerobic photosynthetic growth photon uptake was unconstrained, while oxygen uptake was set to 0 mmol gDW-1 h-1. ... Photosynthetic cultures were sparged with 99% N2 and 1% CO2. Cultures were incubated for app
growth_records.notes: Same SMM (succinate + ammonium sole C/N; composition not numerically stated). Anaerobic photosynthetic batch, 30C, ~7 doublings. Succinate 2.5 and ammonium 1 mmol/gDW/h are FBA input constraints from ref 17, not measured; oxygen uptake fixed at 0 and photon uptake unconstrained for the photosynthetic simulation. No experimental mu, doubling time or measured product flux reported in text; no arithmetic performed.
